Still just the timer changed. The G bar didn't do anything either.
Let me think about this differently. I've been trying to click on various objects, but nothing seems to change the source blocks when they're at 5 pixels.
Wait, let me check the initial state of the level. Maybe the source blocks started at 5 pixels, and the goal is to increase them to 14 or some other value. But I don't have access to the initial state.
Actually, let me check the history to see what the initial state was.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the initial state of the level
if history:
    initial_frame = history[0].frame
    seg = initial_frame.segmentation
    print("Initial state objects:")
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y', 'r']:
            b = n['boundary']
            rows = [p[0] for p in b]
            cols = [p[1] for p in b]
            print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
else:
    print("No history available")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
